name: Helen Morgan
abstract: Helen Morgan (née Riggin; August 2, 1900 – October 9, 1941) was an American singer and actress who worked in films and on the stage. A quintessential torch singer, she made a big splash in the Chicago club scene in the 1920s.

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Now are you familiar with Helen Morgan? ... Helen Morgan was what you would call a chanteuse. She was, she sang in the posh nightclubs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: I remember this one girl... Helen Morgan. She was up there more than anybody else.

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: Helen Morgan and all those kind of people used to come there.
